name: java
description: Java language power pack — modern Java idioms (records, sealed types, var, switch expressions), concurrency safety, null handling, and common JVM pitfalls
rules: Prefer records for immutable data carriers over POJOs with getters/setters
Use sealed interfaces for closed type hierarchies; enables exhaustive pattern matching
Avoid raw types — always parameterize generics
Never catch Throwable or Error — catch specific exceptions
Use try-with-resources for every Closeable; never manual close()
Prefer Optional<T> return over null for missing values (not for fields or params)
Use ConcurrentHashMap, not synchronized HashMap — finer-grained locking
Never ignore InterruptedException — rethrow or set Thread.currentThread().interrupt()
Use Objects.equals / requireNonNull / hash helpers over manual null checks
String concatenation in loops → use StringBuilder; keep single concat for readability

checks.description: catch (Throwable) or catch (Error) — swallows JVM errors and InterruptedException
InterruptedException caught without rethrow or interrupt() — breaks cancellation
Thread.sleep inside a loop — consider ScheduledExecutorService
String += in loop — O(n²) allocations; use StringBuilder
System.exit() in library/server code kills the JVM — propagate exceptions instead
e.printStackTrace() writes to stderr — use a logger
Runtime.exec with a single string is shell-parsed — command injection risk
Likely hardcoded API key / password / token literal
ObjectInputStream.readObject on untrusted input enables RCE
SQL string concatenation — use PreparedStatement with parameters
Missing @Override annotation on a method that looks like it overrides
public static non-final field — global mutable state
new Thread(...).start() — prefer ExecutorService for lifecycle & backpressure
